title: gmelina asiatica
intro: Gmelina asiatica, also known as the Indian beech or Gmelina, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Gmelina asiatica can be prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in the form of essential oils and dried extracts.
